message: Gate skips guarded attempts, so a later blocked git-guard attempt can be hidden by an older successful attempt for the same contract.
trigger: A guarded execute attempt with a non-empty git_guard.terminal_reason exists after an older completed attempt for the same approved contract.
fix_direction: Do not fall back behind a guarded attempt. Return the guarded latest matching attempt and make gate execution fail/block when git_guard.terminal_reason is non-empty, or otherwise stop with an explicit git-guard gate error.
evidence: latestCompletedGateExecution scans attempts newest-first, but when it reads a result with git_guard.terminal_reason it executes continue. If attempt_002 is blocked by executor_git_history_mutation and attempt_001 was a prior successful attempt for the same contract, gate will use attempt_001 and may proceed even though the newest execution was explicitly blocked by the guard.

message: The guard does not snapshot commit objects or non-HEAD reflogs, allowing some git-history mutations to pass as clean.
trigger: During a write-enabled stage, the agent creates a dangling commit object without moving HEAD, refs, or the index, for example with git commit-tree; similarly, it can move a non-HEAD ref away and back so the final ref tips match while that ref's reflog changed.
fix_direction: Snapshot all commit object IDs using git cat-file --batch-all-objects filtered to commits, and snapshot reflog state for HEAD plus every ref. Compare those after the transport and classify HEAD-unchanged object/reflog changes as executor_git_guard_inconclusive with restoration suppressed.
evidence: gitGuardTakeSnapshot only records reflog hints for HEAD and refs/stash, and never records the set of commit object IDs. gitGuardCompareAndRestore then compares only HEAD hash/reflog, refs, and index before returning a clean outcome when detectedMuts is empty.

message: Gate can ignore the latest git-guard-blocked execution by falling back to an older successful attempt.
trigger: A current-contract execution attempt with non-empty git_guard.terminal_reason exists after an older successful current-contract attempt, and the user runs pactum gate run.
fix_direction: Do not continue past the newest matching execution attempt when it has a git-guard terminal reason; surface a gate-blocking state/error/report for that attempt so the guard failure blocks gate progression.
evidence: latestCompletedGateExecution iterates newest to oldest, but a matching result with GitGuard.TerminalReason is skipped with continue. If attempt_002 is blocked by executor_git_history_mutation and attempt_001 is an earlier clean result for the same contract, gate can select attempt_001 and proceed instead of blocking the guarded failure.

message: The guard never snapshots commit object IDs, so commit-object-only history mutations can pass as clean.
trigger: During execute or review-fix, the agent creates a commit object directly, for example with git commit-tree, without updating refs, HEAD, or the index.
fix_direction: Add a snapshot field for the set or digest of existing commit object IDs using git cat-file --batch-all-objects filtered to commits, compare it after transport, and classify new/pruned commit objects without a safe restoration path as executor_git_guard_inconclusive.
evidence: gitGuardTakeSnapshot records HEAD, refs, limited reflog hints, and the index tree, and gitGuardCompareAndRestore compares only those values. There is no git cat-file --batch-all-objects commit set/digest, so a direct dangling commit object is invisible.

message: Reflog detection is incomplete and can miss reflog-only mutations.
trigger: The agent advances and restores history using existing commits, such as reset --hard to the previous commit and then back to the original commit, leaving final HEAD/ref tips unchanged while the reflog has new entries.
fix_direction: Snapshot and compare a stable reflog count or digest for HEAD and every ref with a reflog; when the reflog advances but final refs/HEAD are unchanged, record executor_git_guard_inconclusive and suppress restoration.
evidence: The snapshot stores only HEAD, HEAD@{1}, and optionally refs/stash tip; compare uses only those HEAD values. It does not store a reflog count/hash for HEAD or all refs as required, so reflogs can advance while these sampled object IDs end unchanged.

message: Prechecks miss loose ref lock files, so unsupported locked ref state can still run the agent.
trigger: A stale loose ref lock file exists under .git/refs before execute or review-fix starts.
fix_direction: Detect *.lock files under the refs hierarchy, accounting for the repository git-dir/common-dir layout, and return executor_git_guard_inconclusive before running the transport.
evidence: The stale-lock check only stats index.lock, refs.lock, and packed-refs.lock at the git-dir root. Git loose ref locks such as refs/heads/main.lock or refs/tags/v1.lock are not checked.

message: Git-guard lifecycle behavior is not covered by integration tests.
trigger: When git guard behavior is exercised through `pactum execute run`, `pactum review fix run`, `pactum gate run`, or `pactum review fix apply` rather than direct helper calls.
fix_direction: Add integration tests with temporary git repositories and helper agents that cover execute and review-fix guard outcomes through the real lifecycle, including artifact fields, CLI JSON, next suppression, gate blocking, review-fix apply blocking, and read-only stage non-interference.
evidence: The new lifecycle wrapper runs prechecks, may skip transport, writes git_guard into result artifacts, and suppresses next affordances, but `rg` finds GitGuard test references only in `internal/app/git_guard_test.go`, where tests call helper functions directly. No test drives execute/review-fix through the lifecycle to assert result.json, last-result.json, CLI JSON, next suppression, gate filtering, review-fix apply blocking, or read-only non-interference required by the contract.

message: Several contract-required git-guard edge cases are missing focused tests.
trigger: When the guard encounters one of the untested precondition, combined-mutation, restore-failure, or transport-error-after-mutation paths.
fix_direction: Add `TestGitGuard*` cases using real temporary git repositories for the missing precondition variants, restore failure detail and precedence, inconclusive suppression of all restoration when combined with restorable mutations, and transport error after mutation.
evidence: `git_guard_test.go` covers clean/dirty/detached/merge/index.lock/submodule plus basic commit, index, stash, reset-hard, ref, and review-fix staging cases. It does not test linked worktrees, sparse/split index preconditions, unmerged/cherry-pick/rebase/revert/bisect/sequencer variants, restore failure reporting/precedence, combined inconclusive-plus-restorable mutation suppression, or transport-error-after-mutation handling, all of which are explicitly listed in the approved acceptance criteria.

message: The gate silently skips a git-guard-blocked attempt and can fall back to an older completed attempt for the same contract.
trigger: A current-contract execution attempt with a non-empty git_guard.terminal_reason exists after an earlier completed attempt for the same approved contract.
fix_direction: Treat the newest current-contract attempt with a git-guard terminal reason as gate-blocking instead of continuing to older attempts; return an error or a gate report state that prevents progression.
evidence: latestCompletedGateExecution iterates attempts newest to oldest, but when a matching attempt result has git_guard.terminal_reason set it executes continue, allowing an older matching attempt to be selected and gated.

message: The user-facing write-stage docs were not updated for the new git-history guard behavior.
trigger: Whenever a user runs or automates pactum execute run or pactum review fix run after this change.
fix_direction: Update user-facing workflow/agent docs to describe execute preconditions, review-fix baseline behavior, git_guard result JSON fields, terminal_reason values, and how guard failures suppress next affordances, gate progression, and review-fix apply.
evidence: docs/agents.md still describes write stages as auto-approved with only the ACP scope guard, while the implementation now wires GitGuard into execute and review-fix, emits git_guard result fields, and suppresses next/gate/apply when terminal_reason is non-empty. Searches over README.md, docs/, and assets/agent-skills/pactum found no git_guard or executor_git_ documentation.
